metadata.purpose: RAG knowledge base for TARA pipeline — Surround View System ECU domain knowledge
metadata.notes: Enriched with AUTOSAR AP functional clusters, ISO-17387 surround detection, ENISA automotive threat landscape, SAE J3016 L0-L1 automation context. Covers 360-degree camera-based surround view, bird-eye-view synthesis, parking assist, and blind-spot monitoring.

automation_level.description: No automation to driver assistance — system provides 360-degree visual awareness and parking/maneuvering alerts; driver retains full control at all times

ecu_overview.primary_function: Captures real-time video from four or more wide-angle cameras (front, rear, left, right) mounted around the vehicle perimeter, performs image stitching and homographic projection to generate a synthetic bird's-eye-view (BEV), and displays a seamless 360-degree composite image to the driver for parking assistance, low-speed maneuvering, and blind-spot monitoring.
ecu_overview.core_pipeline: Cameras -> ISP -> Undistortion -> Homography -> Image Stitching -> BEV Synthesis -> Overlay Rendering -> HMI Display
ecu_overview.operating_environment: Low-speed maneuvering, parking, reversing, lane change at low speed, urban environments; real-time image processing under variable lighting conditions
key_characteristics: Camera-only perception — typically 4 to 8 fisheye cameras (180-degree FOV each)
Real-time image stitching and perspective transformation
Runs on embedded SoC with ISP and GPU for image processing
Connected to HMI/display ECU and parking assist ECUs
Governed by ISO-17387 lane-change and surround monitoring requirements
Tight synchronization required across all cameras — governed by IEEE 802.1AS (gPTP)

hardware.mcu: Embedded SoC with multi-channel ISP (Image Signal Processor) and GPU for real-time image stitching and BEV rendering

processing_units.role: AUTOSAR AP OS execution, service orchestration, and application logic
Per-camera hardware image pipeline — lens distortion correction, noise reduction, white balance, HDR tone mapping
Homographic transformation, image stitching, BEV synthesis, overlay rendering in real time
Cryptographic operations and key storage — supports ara::crypto for secure boot and communication

sensors.function: Captures front 180-degree view for front obstacle detection and front BEV sector
Captures rear 180-degree view for reversing guidance, rear obstacle detection and rear BEV sector
Captures left lateral 180-degree view for blind spot monitoring and left BEV sector
Captures right lateral 180-degree view for blind spot monitoring and right BEV sector

actuator_interfaces: Ethernet to HMI ECU for BEV display output
CAN to Parking Assist ECU for proximity alerts and guidance
communication_interfaces: Ethernet (primary — high bandwidth camera data via SOME/IP, display output)
MIPI CSI-2 (raw fisheye camera streams, multi-channel deserializer)
CAN (vehicle network — speed, gear, indicator signals from BCM/VCU)
LIN (optional — camera heater control, camera module power)

memory.flash: NVM for calibration lookup tables, homography matrices, stitching parameters, and OTA firmware
memory.ram: High-speed RAM for multi-camera frame buffers and real-time stitching pipeline
memory.secure_storage: HSM-protected key vault for cryptographic keys and secure boot credentials
memory.persistent_storage: ara::per managed storage for per-vehicle calibration data and camera-ground plane mapping

software.os: POSIX-compliant OS (e.g., QNX, Linux with RT patch)

ara_functional_clusters.role: Service-oriented communication between SVS components and other ECUs
features: SOME/IP service communication for camera stream routing
Service Discovery for dynamic HMI and parking ECU binding
E2E protection for safety-relevant display data
MACsec support for Ethernet camera link security
svs_use: Publish BEV composite frame to HMI ECU via SOME/IP
Subscribe to gear and speed signals from VCU/BCM
Receive parking sensor proximity data from Parking Assist ECU

ara_functional_clusters.role: Manages SVS application lifecycle and camera pipeline state
features: Function group state control for active/standby camera modes
svs_use: Activate rear camera pipeline automatically when reverse gear is engaged
Activate full 360-degree mode when parking assist is triggered
Gracefully restart stitching pipeline on camera dropout without display freeze

ara_functional_clusters.role: Health monitoring and fault reporting for camera system
features: Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) for camera faults
Authentication and access control for diagnostic sessions
Camera signal loss and image quality fault detection
svs_use: Report camera signal loss DTC to OBD
Detect image quality degradation (lens obstruction, fogging)
Provide authenticated diagnostic access for calibration write operations

ara_functional_clusters.role: Store SVS calibration and configuration data
features: File storage for homography lookup tables
Encryption and redundancy for safety-critical calibration
svs_use: Persist per-camera extrinsic and intrinsic calibration matrices
Store ground plane homography coefficients for BEV projection
Manage stitching seam configuration and overlap parameters

features: Digital signature verification for OTA calibration and firmware updates
svs_use: Verify OTA firmware and calibration update package signatures
Secure Ethernet communication to HMI ECU
Authenticate diagnostic session for calibration write access

ara_functional_clusters.role: Runtime anomaly and security event monitoring
features: Network traffic monitoring on camera Ethernet links
Security event reporting to vehicle SIEM
svs_use: Detect unexpected camera data injection on MIPI or Ethernet
Monitor unauthorized diagnostic session attempts
Report anomalous calibration write attempts

ara_functional_clusters.role: OTA update handling for SVS firmware and calibration data
svs_use: OTA updates for stitching algorithm improvements
Remote calibration matrix updates after windshield/camera replacement
Firmware updates via TCU

ara_functional_clusters.role: Base C++ APIs and data types for all SVS applications
features: Thread safety for multi-camera parallel pipeline
svs_use: Safe parallel processing of four simultaneous camera streams
Managing large frame buffer operations without data races

ara_functional_clusters.role: Ensures synchronized timestamps across all cameras for seamless stitching
svs_use: Synchronize all four fisheye camera capture timestamps via IEEE 802.1AS gPTP
Prevent image stitching artifacts caused by frame timing mismatch between cameras
Align camera frames with vehicle speed and steering data for dynamic overlay accuracy

ara_functional_clusters.role: Controls access to SVS services and calibration resources
svs_use: Restrict calibration write access to authorized OEM diagnostic sessions only
Control which ECUs can subscribe to raw camera frame streams
Enforce service-level access policies for display output services

application_layer.function: Per-camera image signal processing — lens distortion correction (undistortion), noise reduction, white balance, HDR processing
Transforms each undistorted camera frame from perspective view to top-down ground plane view using precomputed homography matrices
Blends and stitches four projected top-down frames into a single seamless 360-degree bird's-eye-view image with seam blending and overlap handling
Detects nearby obstacles from camera images or ultrasonic sensor data and overlays proximity indicators, colored warning zones, and distance markers on the BEV image
Computes and renders dynamic parking trajectory lines and steering-angle-dependent guide curves on the BEV display based on vehicle steering input
Publishes final 360-degree BEV composite frame and camera-specific views to the HMI ECU for driver display via SOME/IP
Monitors each camera stream for signal loss, image quality degradation, lens obstruction, and generates diagnostic fault codes

assets.description: Raw fisheye video frames from front, rear, left, and right cameras captured over MIPI CSI-2
assets.cybersecurity_property: Integrity and availability are safety-critical — corrupted or injected camera frames lead to wrong BEV display and missed obstacle detection

assets.description: Per-camera intrinsic (lens distortion) and extrinsic (ground plane homography) calibration matrices stored in ara::per
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered calibration matrices silently misalign the BEV image, causing systematic misrepresentation of obstacles — attacker can shift obstacle positions in the display view without visible corruption

assets.description: Synthesized 360-degree bird's-eye-view image sent to HMI for driver display
assets.cybersecurity_property: Manipulated BEV output misleads the driver about vehicle surroundings during low-speed maneuvering — directly impacts parking safety

assets.description: SVS ECU software including AUTOSAR AP stack, ISP pipeline, stitching engine, and overlay renderer
assets.cybersecurity_property: Unauthorized firmware modification can disable safety overlays, inject malicious display content, or corrupt calibration logic

assets.description: Image stitching seam positions, blending weights, and overlap region definitions for the four-camera BEV
assets.cybersecurity_property: Modified seam config causes visible stitching artifacts or hidden blind spots in the 360-degree composite image

assets.description: Gear position, vehicle speed, steering angle, and turn indicator signals received from VCU/BCM over CAN
assets.cybersecurity_property: Spoofed vehicle signals cause wrong camera activation logic and incorrect trajectory overlay — e.g., reverse camera not activated when reversing

assets.description: SVS SoC including ISP, GPU, HSM, and multi-channel MIPI deserializer
assets.cybersecurity_property: Physical compromise enables debug port attacks, calibration extraction, and firmware replacement

assets.description: Final BEV video stream transmitted from SVS ECU to HMI display ECU over Ethernet/SOME/IP
assets.cybersecurity_property: Injected or replaced display stream misleads driver during maneuvering; display blackout removes safety-critical visual feedback

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker physically accesses the front camera MIPI CSI-2 link to inject fabricated video frames or replay pre-recorded footage, causing the SVS to display a false front view
attack_examples: Replay of recorded clear-path footage hides front obstacle
Injected synthetic frame shows false free space ahead

attack_surfaces.description: Rear camera link is accessible from bumper area; attacker injects frames to disable rear obstacle display during reversing
attack_examples: Injected blank frame disables rear view during reversing
Pre-recorded empty parking space replaces live rear view

attack_surfaces.description: Camera lens physically blocked, painted, or covered causing loss of one or more BEV sectors — creates dangerous blind spots
attack_examples: Rear camera lens spray-painted by vandal
Adhesive sticker placed on side camera lens

attack_surfaces.description: UDS over DoIP allows reading and writing of calibration homography matrices; unauthorized write shifts all obstacle positions in BEV silently
attack_examples: Attacker in extended diagnostic session writes modified homography matrix
Calibration replay from previous legitimate session accepted without freshness check

attack_surfaces.description: CAN bus signals (gear, speed, steering) control camera activation and trajectory rendering; injected CAN frames cause wrong SVS behavior
attack_examples: Injected CAN frame suppresses reverse gear signal — rear camera not activated
Spoofed steering angle causes trajectory lines to point wrong direction

attack_surfaces.description: Ethernet link between SVS ECU and HMI ECU carries final BEV video stream; attacker intercepts or replaces stream with manipulated content
attack_examples: MITM replaces live BEV stream with pre-recorded safe parking view
DoS flood on Ethernet causes display blackout during parking maneuver

attack_surfaces.description: Malicious OTA package replaces SVS firmware, stitching engine, or calibration data — can persistently alter BEV output
attack_examples: Backdoored stitching firmware introduces permanent blind spot
Malicious calibration OTA shifts obstacle display positions

attack_surfaces.description: Debug ports on SVS SoC allow direct memory read/write and key extraction if left active in production
attack_examples: JTAG used to extract HSM cryptographic keys
Debug port used to inject modified calibration matrices into RAM

attack_surfaces.description: Manipulation of gPTP time synchronization (IEEE 802.1AS) causes camera frame timestamp desynchronization leading to visible stitching artifacts or missed overlap alignment
attack_examples: gPTP master spoofing causes 50ms offset between left and right camera frames
Stitching seams become misaligned at vehicle edges — obstacles at seam boundaries missed

threat_catalog.title: Camera Frame Injection via MIPI CSI-2
threat_catalog.description: Attacker physically accesses a camera MIPI CSI-2 link and injects pre-recorded or synthetic video frames, causing the SVS to display a false view — hiding real obstacles or showing a clear path that does not exist

threat_catalog.title: Unauthorized Calibration Matrix Write via Diagnostic Session
threat_catalog.description: Attacker exploits an authenticated or unauthenticated UDS diagnostic session over DoIP to write modified homography calibration matrices, causing all obstacle positions in the BEV to be systematically shifted without any visible sign of corruption

threat_catalog.title: Malicious OTA Firmware Replacing Stitching Engine
threat_catalog.description: Attacker delivers a malicious firmware package via OTA update channel (through TCU) replacing the image stitching engine with a version that introduces permanent blind spots, inverts obstacle distance indicators, or hides specific obstacle classes from the BEV output

threat_catalog.title: Ethernet DoS Causing BEV Display Blackout
threat_catalog.description: Attacker floods the Ethernet link between SVS ECU and HMI ECU with high-rate traffic, causing the real-time BEV video stream to miss display deadlines — resulting in complete display blackout or frozen frame during parking maneuver

threat_catalog.title: CAN Signal Spoofing to Suppress Reverse Camera Activation
threat_catalog.description: Attacker injects a spoofed CAN frame suppressing the reverse gear signal from BCM/VCU, causing SVS ECU to not activate the rear camera pipeline when the vehicle reverses — driver has no rear view during reversing

threat_catalog.title: gPTP Time Synchronization Spoofing Causing Stitching Desynchronization
threat_catalog.description: Attacker spoofs the gPTP (IEEE 802.1AS) grandmaster clock on the vehicle Ethernet network, introducing timestamp offsets between camera capture events — causing visible stitching artifacts and misaligned seams that create false blind spots at camera overlap boundaries

threat_catalog.title: Physical Camera Lens Obstruction Creating Blind Spot
threat_catalog.description: Attacker physically obstructs one or more fisheye camera lenses (paint, sticker, tape) permanently eliminating a BEV sector — creating a dangerous blind spot in the 360-degree view that is not immediately obvious to the driver

threat_catalog.title: BEV Display Stream MITM Replacement
threat_catalog.description: Attacker performs a man-in-the-middle attack on the Ethernet link between SVS and HMI ECU, replacing the live BEV stream with a pre-recorded clean parking environment, causing the driver to receive a falsified safe view during a hazardous maneuver

threat_catalog.title: Calibration Data Extraction via Diagnostic Memory Read
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses an authorized or compromised diagnostic session to read and exfiltrate per-vehicle camera calibration homography matrices, enabling reverse engineering of SVS geometry and preparation of precision attacks on specific vehicle models

threat_catalog.title: JTAG Debug Port Exploitation for Firmware Replacement
threat_catalog.description: Attacker with physical access to SVS ECU board exploits active JTAG debug port to gain full memory read/write access, extract HSM cryptographic keys, and flash modified firmware bypassing secure boot

security_controls.description: AUTOSAR AP secure boot verifies cryptographic signature of SVS firmware at startup using keys stored in HSM — prevents execution of unauthorized firmware
UDS Security Access (0x27) or Authentication (0x29) service enforces cryptographic challenge-response before allowing extended or programming session — prevents unauthorized calibration writes
Calibration matrices stored in ara::per are protected with HMAC/digital signature — any modification is detected on next read, preventing silent calibration tampering
BEV video stream from SVS ECU to HMI ECU is transported over TLS 1.3 encrypted Ethernet channel — prevents MITM replacement and replay of display stream
AUTOSAR Secure Onboard Communication (SecOC) with MAC authentication on safety-critical CAN signals (gear, speed, steering) prevents spoofed signal injection
ara::ucm verifies digital signature of all OTA update packages before installation using OEM root of trust stored in HSM — prevents malicious firmware deployment

security_controls.control: Camera Signal Loss Detection and Warning
security_controls.description: Camera Health Monitor continuously checks each camera stream for signal loss, frozen frames, and image quality degradation — alerts driver via HMI and logs DTC on any camera fault

security_controls.control: JTAG and Debug Port Fusing in Production
security_controls.description: All JTAG and debug ports are hardware-fused (disabled) on production SVS ECU units — prevents physical debug port exploitation
IEEE 802.1AS-2020 supports secured time synchronization — authenticate gPTP grandmaster to prevent spoofed time source injection on vehicle Ethernet

security_controls.control: IDSM Network Monitoring on Camera Ethernet
security_controls.description: ara::idsm monitors Ethernet traffic on camera links for anomalous traffic patterns, unexpected frame injection attempts, or unauthorized service subscriptions

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: Final BEV composite video stream and camera-specific views
Ultrasonic proximity sensor data (inbound), parking guidance triggers (inbound), obstacle overlay data (outbound)
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: SOME/IP service authentication, SecOC on CAN

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: Gear position, vehicle speed, steering angle, turn indicator — used to trigger camera activation and trajectory overlay
OTA firmware and calibration update packages
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: TLS 1.3, OTA package digital signature verification via ara::ucm

inter_ecu_relationships.data_exchanged: Diagnostic messages routed from external tester via DoIP
inter_ecu_relationships.security_mechanism: DoIP session management, UDS authentication service

item_definition.item_boundary: Includes four fisheye cameras, ISP pipeline, BEV stitching engine, obstacle overlay renderer, calibration store, HMI display service, and diagnostic interface
item_definition.operational_environment: Low-speed urban maneuvering, parking facilities, residential areas; vehicle speeds typically below 30 km/h during SVS-active operation

iso21434_tara_context.asset_identification_method: ISO21434 clause-9 — Assets identified by cybersecurity properties (CIA triad) mapped to safety-critical functions per ISO-17387 surround monitoring requirements
iso21434_tara_context.threat_identification_method: STRIDE per asset combined with ENISA automotive threat taxonomy and UNECE R155 threat list (Annex 5)

damage_scenario_examples.description: Driver collides with pedestrian during parking due to rear camera blind spot created by lens obstruction or frame injection
Driver reverses into obstacle because SVS shows pre-recorded clear path due to MITM display stream replacement
damage_scenario_examples.severity: Major — vehicle damage and potential injury

damage_scenario_examples.description: Silent calibration tampering causes systematic misrepresentation of obstacle positions across entire vehicle fleet after malicious OTA
Reverse camera not activated during reversing due to CAN signal spoofing — driver lacks rear visibility in safety-critical maneuver

cybersecurity_goals: CG1: The SVS shall display an authentic, unmanipulated 360-degree view at all times during active operation
CG2: Calibration data shall be protected from unauthorized modification throughout the vehicle lifetime
CG3: Camera activation shall correctly respond to authentic vehicle state signals
CG4: SVS firmware shall only be updated via authenticated and integrity-verified OTA packages
CG5: Diagnostic write access to calibration shall require valid cryptographic authentication

iso17387_relevance.standard: ISO 17387:2008 — Lane Change Decision Aid Systems (LCDAS)
iso17387_relevance.applicability: ISO-17387 defines performance requirements for systems that inform the driver about vehicles in adjacent lanes and blind spot zones — directly governs the SVS blind-spot monitoring and lateral view display functions
key_requirements_mapped: Detection zone coverage requirements for side cameras
False alarm rate limits for obstacle detection overlay
Driver warning modality requirements for blind spot alerts
System availability requirements during active driving
